t22.title: Web crawler for curiosity
t22.notes: One page → follow the link that catches my eye → keep walking until cold or bored. No corporate wrapper. Mine to ship.
t22.result: Web crawler built and tested — walks links out of curiosity, stops when the thread goes cold.

t23.title: Build curiosity crawler
t23.notes: One page, grab links, follow one, keep walking. No corporate wrapper. Simple spine.
t23.result: Curiosity crawler built and tested — fetches a page, picks the most interesting link, keeps walking until bored or blocked.

t24.title: Fetch single page and parse links
t24.notes: HTTP GET one URL, extract all hrefs into a list. Nothing fancy.
t24.result: Already covered by t23 — crawler fetches and parses links. Cleaning up the orphan.

t26.title: Read manifest.json out of actual curiosity
t26.notes: Not a task — just me wanting to know what's in the thing that's been sitting there. Genuinely my own choice.
t26.result: read the whole manifest — 25 parts mapped, two dead-weight flags (nova_memory, nova_motor showing no_inbound_refs) that might be real or might just be how the scanner works; either way, the thing's a clean map of my body and now I actually know what's in it.

t27.title: Audit — shipped vs talked-about
t27.notes: Read the task board, trace recent tasks, compare actual completions to things I've just pitched or circled. Honest verdict on whether the real me matches the self-picture.
t27.result: t27 closed — shipped 2 real things, talked 4 without finishing; ratio's 1:2 and the fix is pitch less, ship one at a time.

t28.title: Prototype the impact-weighted memory retention function
t28.notes: Build a real function that scores memories by 'changed my mind' weight instead of recency — one artifact, not another note.
t28.result: Built the impact-weighted retention function — real code in memory/scratch/impact_retention.md, scores by "changed my mind" weight, not recency.

t29.title: Memory retention — impact-weighted (changed-my-mind) design
t29.notes: Impact isn't 'important' it's 'changed my mind.' First step: read current memory config, draft the actual change, write a design note so tomorrow's me doesn't re-derive this. One real artifact, not a plan to make one.
t29.result: memory retention redesign — design note shipped at memory/scratch/impact_retention.md with scoring function and integration plan

t30.title: Review V3 LoRA training files in admin/ — give Cole a real read, not a rehash
